name: read_tickets_tool
summary: Read tickets from the queue: look up by key, list by status or assignee, or search task bodies.
Returns a markdown ticket block (`get`) or a bulleted summary list (`list`, `search`).
constraints: `get` defaults `key` to the agent's current ticket — omit `key` to read your own work.
`list` and `search` cap at 50 tickets per call; tighten filters rather than re-running.
anti_patterns: To create or edit tickets, or settle one with `done` — use `write_tickets_tool`.
When the agent needs read AND write access in the same flow — use `manage_tickets_tool` instead of registering both.
To find code or files in the working directory — use `grep_tool` / `glob_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: tickets the agent has not yet picked up have no `assignee` — `list` with `assignee` filter excludes them.
IMPORTANT: `search` matches the task body only — labels and result text are not searched.
output: `get`: a markdown block with key, status, reporter, assignee, labels, task body, and result.

action.description: Read mode: `get` (one ticket), `list` (filter by status / assignee), `search` (free-text).

key.description: Ticket key for `get` (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Defaults to the agent's current ticket. Ignored by `list` / `search`.

status.description: Filter for `list`: one of `Todo`, `InProgress`, `Done`, `Failed`. Combine with `assignee` to narrow further.

assignee.description: Filter for `list`: only tickets assigned to this name. Tickets with no assignee are never returned by this filter.

query.description: Free-text query for `search`. Case-insensitive substring match against the task body.
